The house is located a few streets from the main road so it is super peaceful and quite at night. The location is convenient as well. You will be able to easily find public transits, restaurants, grocery stores or parks all within 20-30 mins walking distance.

Hi Toshiyuki,
We had such a lovely stay in your unit in Tokyo.
From the moment we arrived, everything was clean,and thoughfully prepared.
Specially your kitchen with all equioment and other staff.
The location was perfect-close to public transpot, restaurant , cafe and shops.
You was incredib;y kind and responsive, making sure we had everythung we needed.
Thank you for making our trip so memorable.
Kind regards,
Irina & Alex

la casa se ubica en una zona muy tranquila y linda. esta cerca de varias estaciones de metro, lo que lo hace conveniente para tener distintas rutas. también esta cerca de la estación que te lleva al aeropuerto de Narita, lo que lo hizo muy conveniente para nosotros que salimos de ahí, y no nos habíamos percatado antes. El baño se comprate, con más de una persona y cada día nos encontramos con alguien distinto al interior, nos hubiera gustado entender eso en un principio, lo que hacía que se sintiera más como un hostal. Fuera de eso, todo estuvo bien, Toshi es amable y creo que es un gran lugar para conocer otra zona de la ciudad.
Great stay! Exactly as described. Toshi is a very friendly, responsive and helpful host.
Wonderful location and great hosts! I had a good time staying in Toshi’s place and it was super close to University of Tokyo, which saved me a lot of hustle to travel to the conference.

The room was super clean and cozy, and the facilities were really nice. I got along really well with my roommates, especially Toshi – he was so friendly and made us feel really welcome. We had some great chats with him about Japan’s culture and economy. I totally fell in love with this neighborhood – it’s so pretty and peaceful. We kicked off our Tokyo trip here, wandered around all these little streets, and I really hope I can come back someday. Definitely recommend staying here!
I had a wonderful stay here. Toshi was incredibly nice and accommodating. The space was comfortable and I enjoyed my time here very much. I highly recommend staying here!

My stay with Toshi was fantastic. The space is very nice, and the neighborhood is wonderful, everyone was very friendly. Toshi had some great recommendations too of local places to try! I hope to stay here again when I come back to Tokyo.

This is a quite spacious, well equipped and very clean apartment. I like mostly is it's sunny balcony. You can dry the washing under the bright sun.
The traffic is very convenient, 15 min. walk to the metro station, just behind the famous Asakusa Temple.
And Tony is a great host and an excellent guides, knows everything about Tokyo, has given us many helpful suggestion. He picked us up at the first day when we arrived and drove us to the station at the last day.
We didn't have much time to talk, but three night short talk make us good friends.
Thank Tony and his wife. I will recommend my friends to stay in their apartment in the future.

Rayleigh

We had a great stay at Tony's place. It was spacious for 2 of us. Tony was really kind to pick us up from the train station and was extremely patience as we arrived half an hour late from our appointed time. The place is clean, we could do our laundry with space to dry them out as well. A comfortable and quiet area to rest after a long day exploring the city. The walk to the train station takes about 15-20 mins walk. But it's great to see the Sensoji temple at night and the Skytree tower day and night while walking out. The apartment is on level 4 (3rd floor), with no lift. But Tony was really kind in helping us carrying up (and down) our super heavy luggage. And he even send us to the train station on the day we left which was 6 am. Can't express my gratitude enough and Tony's kindness. Thank you Tony for a memorable stay.

Tony-san is a very kind and friendly host who waited for us at the bus-stop on our first day in Tokyo and gave us useful directions. The apt is clean, tidy, balcony is good for laundry, near Asakusa station (Ginza line), 10-15 mins walk to Sensoji, the Sky Tree, and a 30mins bus ride to JR Tokyo station. Very nice walks to and fro and very convenient to the places we wanted to go. Do note that the room is on the 4th floor so there might be a bit of hassle dragging large luggages. On the last day he also very kindly drove us to Asakusa station, which has a direct train to NEL, which we initally did not realise. Very happy and grateful and would want to stay in Asakusa again!

Tony was one of the kindest and most welcoming Airbnb hosts I have ever stayed with. He offered to pick us up from and drive us to the train station so we wouldn't have to carry our bags, offered us umbrellas when it was raining, and invited us to share drinks and stories with him and his wife. He also gave us great advice about Tokyo and Japan. Tony is the ideal Airbnb host, and you should definitely stay with him!
The place is well located (you can see the sky tree from the balcony), clean, secure. And Tony, the owner, is a nice and hepful person ! Thank you we spend a wonderfull sejour in Asakusa.

Wir haben uns sehr willkommen gefühlt! Wir wurden ( zum Glück ) von Toni in Asakusa Station abgeholt, somit hatten wir auch kein Problem die Unterkunft zu finden. Alles was wir brauchten war da und es war super zentral. Für den Preis alles perfekt =) Danke!

Hôte et appartement très agréables
Tony est venu nous chercher au bus et nous a ramené au métro pour repartir
Disponible, présent, très sympa et parlant bien anglais
Asakusa est un quartier très chouette, à échelle humaine, et l'appartement est à 10 min du métro Asakusa qui va partout.
Nombreux commerces
Belle rencontre de Tony et sa femme qui aiment discuter et échanger
A recommander

Arriving to the station, Tony promptly came upon notifying our arrival. He was very helpful with our luggages. It was rather very comforting to get into his heated car as it was very cold outside!

We were straight shown to our room which was spacious, well equipped for a proper stay. Tony showed us around the room in a thorough manner which was very nice. In addition to that, he even gave directions for the area.

Throughout the whole stay, he kept giving us advices and tips on what and where to visit, how to get around and such.

As for the location, it was a short walk; approximately 15-20 mins to the Asakusa station or just a short bus ride. Plenty of convenience stores and also very close the all famous Sensoji Temple.

Tony is an ideal host and we would definitely be back if we were to come back to Tokyo for another holiday!

Tony was a great host, he gave us directions how to get to Asakusa station and picked us up there. The apartment has everything you need and the softest futon beds I've tried. From leaving the apartment to arriving at shibuya station takes about 50 minutes so it's okay.

เจ้าของใจดีมากเป็นกันเอง ห้องพักอยู่ชั้น4 ไม่มีลิฟท์แต่ก็มาช่วยขนกระเป๋าให้ใกล้วัดเซ็นโซจิ สถานีรถใต้ดินที่ใกล้ที่สุด asakusa แต่ก็ไกลนิดนึงใช้เวลาเดิน15-20นาทีได้ แต่เดินบ่อยๆ ก็รู้สึกชิน เจ้าของจะพาเราเดินไปรถไฟใต้ดินและบอกวิธีการสังเกตุเพื่อให้เราจำทางได้ ตอนกลับก็ช่วยขนของและขับรถไปส่งที่สถานีรถไฟด้วย

Tony made us feel very welcome by walking with us from the bus stop to his lovely apartment, carrying our heavy luggage up the many stairs and then driving us to the airport on the way home, the description was accurate (very large place) and the neighbourhood was very safe and quiet but perhaps a little far from train stations if you frequently go to the city (it's possible to catch a bus but we preferred to walk)
Tony and his wife are extremely friendly and lovely person , we had very good experience with them , location isn't bad if you can find the nearest way

Tony 是非常好的民宿主人。
在我們出發到日本前，
便非常熱心提供協助，
到日本後，準時出現在約定的地點，
接我們到他的民宿。
在日本的每一天，
他還會細心詢問我們的行程
並給予最佳化的建議。
此次的東京行，很高興能住在他的民宿~
一個乾淨、整齊的空間，
令人覺得舒服、自在。
謝謝你~Tony
ありがとうございます。
私は再び来る。

Hirano-san was extremely gracious and helpful in offering to pick us up from the subway station and help us with our luggage, which was a real plus, as the apartment is not walking distance from the subway if you have bags.  However, once you are settled in, it is close to two bus stops for either Ueno or Asakusa, which makes it a very useful and convenient spot in old Edo.  It is only a few blocks from the temple area pictured in the photo along with numerous shops and restaurants, and yet is nestled in a quiet neighborhood that allows you to really experience the real Japan including your own deep hot bath.  We especially treasured the view of Tokyo Skytree at night from the balcony.  Make sure to try sushi at Kintaro around the corner.  It is a historic spot and has potentially the best sushi in the world.  Awesome spot, incredible bargain!
Un accueil parfait. Tony Hirano-San nous a permis de découvrir le Tokyo des Tokyoites et en particulier de ceux d'Asakusa.

Incredible stay in Tokyo with Tony and Yoko! Tony came to meet us at the bus stop close to his place and showed us our apartment in the same building where he lives. The apartment is big and very confortable, japanese style bedroom(tatami and futons) with A/C, big living room and shower box. We stayed like at home! Tony and Yoko were very nice and helpful. Upon arrival Tony had ready a very cute home-style maps with the most relevant attractions, restaurants and facilities in the area. Asakusa is a very moving area during thesay, quiet at night. We went with them to the local onsen, simply beautiful. And to a typical restaurant for meat in a bowl and fried pork. Our stay was really great. Thank you and see you soon!

Hirano's place in Asakusa was the perfect start to our trip around Japan. It's comfortable, convenient, nice, clean and well equiped. This has been our best AirBnB experience so far, and we highly recomend the place to anyone looking to visit Tokyo.

We were travelling with 2 baby kids and the place is perfect for families. If you're travelling with kids you should consider booking this place.

Hirano speaks excellent english, and he is dedicated to taking care of the house and the guests; if it's your first time to Japan, he will be a huge help. If you want to chat to him he'll happily invite you into his house and feed you ice-cold Sake. Thanks Hirano!

A great stay at Tony's place. The appartment is spacious and comfortable, very conveniently located near a bus stop and many retaurants and shops.
Tony is the best host ever !

Tony is a nice and helpful person. Upon we have booked his apartment, he helped us to check the schedule of the train from Narita to Asakusa. He also took us by his car from the station to his apartment even though we have arrived at the Asakusa Station at 23:30. He also took us to the station when we left. He also gave us subjections for our trip. His apartment is tidy and comfortable so that we have the feeling as stay at home.

Hirano san was very helpfull. He always tried to advise us from the very same time we made the booking. We felt like home in his place.

The flat was spacious and comfortable for us four, with a fully equiped kitchen, bathroom and japanese style bedroom. It was extremelly clean. The location is very quiet but at the same time surrounded of all basic services and close to a bus stop.

Hirano san helped us with our backpack giving us a ride at our arrival and departure. He and his wife were very kind welcoming us with a japanese dinner and a nice and interesting talk.

Arigatoo!

Tony san and his wife are really great people, hospitable, discreet and ready to help. the apartment is practical and functional. the area is quiet and pleasant. the shops are close. Metro Station is convenient, the bus stops are a 3 minute walk and you can even take the shuttle boat. Asakusa is really great! Tony San thank you a great month of September in part thanks to you.

Tony was an amazing host. He helped us wth our bags, helped us modify our travel plans at the last minute because of inclement weather in another location, and he has the sweetest granddaughters who happily played with our infant son and gave us some time to breathe. Tony and his incredibly hospitable wife also took us out for some amazing ramen, a completely unexpected joy. It was really the best stay I have ever had.
About the room, it was kept at the highest level of cleanliness and was well stocked with everything we needed to cook and stay settled. And as the rooms are set up in a more traditional manner with sleeping arrangements on the ground, it was just so easy for our son and us. Even without a baby bed, we knew he couldn't fall out or get hurt in the room. The Internet was fast and without issue, and any questions we had Tony immediately helped us.
For any future travel to Tokyo, I think we have our go to apartment. I could easily imagine staying here for several weeks or even months if we go for a longer period of time. It was that nice. This was possibly the best introduction to Airbnb I could hope for.

Tony is the perfect host. He picked us from asakusa station and showed us the neighborhood. We had even the opportunity to share lunch and dinner with Tony and his lovely family.
The apartment is ideally located if you look for an authentic place to stay. Asakusa is a really lovely neighborhood (10 minutes walking to main shrine and subways and 3 minutes from bus to Tokyo station). The flat is very functional, clean and super quiet.
Thank you so much Tony for your kindness and hospitality.

Nous avons passez deux semaines dans cet appartement, tout était parfait, conforme aux photos, très bien placé dans le quartier d'asakusa qui est magnifique (surtout la nuit)  il y a un arrêt de bus à quelque mètre de la qui vous emmène  a la gare qui elle vous emmène partout ! Hirano est quelqu'un de très gentil et de tres serviable, la communication avec lui est tres bonne. Je recommande fortement cet endroit

We had a wonderful time at Tony's apartment and Tony is by far the kindest host we've met on AirBnb. We were staying in a hostel prior to checking in to Tony's place and he knew that we had to check out of our hostel at 10am. He offered to pick us up after our check-out and brought us to the apartment so that we could have the full day to enjoy Tokyo. Before arriving at the apartment Tony drove us around the neighbourhood, pointing out the sights and the train station, etc. Tony gave us suggestions for different activities that were happening in Tokyo during our stay and provided hand-drawn maps as well!

After we checked out of Tony's place he stored our bags for us and later drove us to our next place in Tokyo (we would have happily stayed at Tony's for longer if it hadn't been booked!). On the way to our next apartment in Tokyo Tony brought us to watch a tea ceremony, which was a great treat.

The apartment itself is in a quiet location and has everything you need for your stay.

Thank you Tony!
Hirano san is very kind and he have sevice mind. I love this place. I recomment you to stay with him. A lot of sento around this place. Thank you, Stop and friend.

This is the first time we using Airbnb. Tony is our first host and he has been very helpful even before arriving at Tokyo. Once arrived at Tokyo, he picked us up at the Asakusa station and showed us around the place before checking into the apartment. The apartment is clean and have all the necessary household amenities. Tony has drawn up a few maps that were very helpful for first timer to get to the attractions around the area and it took about 20min walked to the Asakusa station.
We have a great time in Tokyo and will highly recommend friends to Tony if they planning to visit Tokyo.

Hirano San is definitely a warmth host. He greeted us at the bus-stop nearby his house on our arrival and helped us with our luggage. After checked-in, he shown us his hand-drawn map of his neighborhood, give us advices on where to find good food and so. He will also check with us if everything is good from time to time during our stay.

His apartment is spacious and comfortable to stay with all the necessity available. He drove us to Tokyo Station upon check out and even offer to keep our big luggage at his place after check out so that we could travel to our next destination more easily. we feel so lucky to stay in his apartment during our trip in Tokyo. Thank you for everything again Hirano-san.

Hirano è un uomo fantastico ed è sempre molto disponibile!peccato non aver avuto più tempo per stare con lui a fargli mille domande. La casa è esattamente come dalle foto. I letti sono molto comodi e puliti. Se soggiornate da lui non avete bisogno di portarvi nulla, asciugamani grandi, shampoo, spazzolini...c'è davvero tutto. In un quarto d'ora a piedi siete nel tempio principale di asakusa e sulla via del ritorno dalla metropolitana ci sono ristoranti e supermercati. Grazie hirano, torneremo sicuramente!

Had a wonderful stay at Tony´s apartment. We were traveling with our 9 month old baby and the flat had everything we needed for a comfortable stay. It was warm, comfy, and quiet. The futon style bed was great and we slept soundly every night. It´s great if you have a baby as there´s no need for a travel cot.
Asakusa is a really interesting area, it´s quieter than central Tokyo and retains the charm of what Tokyo must have been like in older times.
Tony and Kazue are the loveliest hosts, they were out of their way to help us and even offered for us to borrow a pushchair for our son. Its was a life saver as we were traveling light and only had a baby carrier. Tony picked us up from the station and showed us around so we could have an idea about the area, he was always available to answer any questions and we had the chance to spend some time with him and his lovely family.
We had a memorable stay, fell in love with Asakusa and its people and would love to go back in the not so distant future. Japanese hospitality at its best!!

A lovely quiet residential neighbourhood with bus stops to Tokyo Sky Tree, Asakusa, Tokyo and Ueno stations. A nice 10-15 min stroll through the local streets to Hanayashiki theme park, Kaminarimon, Nakamise and surrounded by a variety of great local eateries, including Yakitori, Sushi, Gyoza, Ramen, Tonkatsu and a wide choice of Izakayas, lively throughout the day and night! Lovely, kind and helpful hosts from Mr and Mrs Hirano including their adorable grandchildren who played with our daughter. THANK YOU Hiranosan for making our stay so comfortable and for all your help!!!

It was a wonderful stay at Tony´s. We were traveling with our 1.4 years old baby and the flat had everything we need, even diapers! Amazing~ The room is exactly looks like pictures. Not too spacious but enough for us.
Location-wise, not far from bus stop which can take you to Asakusa or Ueno easily. We love this area.
Tony was a warm and friend-like host. Helped us and even offered us pushchair for our son. Tony picked us up from the station and sent us back with our biggest luggages :p What a life saver!
We had a memorable stay and would love to go back again. Hope Tony's apartment will still be available then!

Hirano-san is a wonderful and welcoming host! He was a great help to my husband and me both before and during our stay and even gave us a lift to the Asakusa station when we were leaving. The Asakusa neighbourhood is a good value for money and not as intimidating as some of the other parts of Tokyo. Some streets are lined with cherry trees, I think of Yaezakura variety, which look absolutely lovely in April.

The apartment itself was spacious and well-lit, equipped with everything we might need during our stay. Tatami bedroom is great for sleeping. The view of Tokyo Skytree at night was also a nice perk. Probably the only problem is that the apartment is situated on the third floor with no elevator - no big deal for us, but might be important for some people.

An excellent place to stay in Tokyo.  We were made to feel very welcome by our host. He met us at the bus stop when we arrived and gave us a ride to the station with all our luggage the day that we left.  Both my daughter and I had birthdays while there and received a gift. The description of the apartment is very accurate.  The Sensouji Temple and subway station were easy to find.  Wakamise is a great place to souvenir shop if you want lots of choice all in the one spot.

Tony's flat was great and looked very much like on the add. Tony picked us up at the station the evening we arrived and made sure we had everything we needed. We felt welcomed and enjoyed our stay there. The flat is located in Asakusa which is a good area to be in. It's in a quiet part of Asakusa as well so you don't have to fear to drunken tourists shouting at night. It's far from the train station (20mn and we're fast walkers) but we enjoyed the walk as you have the possibility of going through nice streets...

We came to Asakusa to spend a relaxed few days in a traditional area of Tokyo. Tony was a great host, providing a wealth of local knowledge and helpful hand-drawn maps. The apartment is very quiet and peaceful and close to all the interesting local sights. There is a new supermarket only 30 seconds walk from the apartment which is very convenient and plenty of restaurants nearby.
Asakusa is a wonderful place, with the temple area, surrounding shops, Skytree and amusement park all within walking distance. I recommend it.

Tony is absolutely great. His house is clean and comfortable,in a peaceful and nice neighbourhood. Is a very helpful host and he makes you feel at home immediately. He came to the station for picking up when arrived and explained perfectly some tricks for moving more efficiently through the city. He kept our luggage while we were visiting Kyoto area and he even drove us to a subway station when returned. A very, very recommended place to stay. We felt very grateful with his treatment. Thanks Tony

Hirano san est venu nous chercher À la gare, aporté nos valises jusqu'à l'appartement, nous avais fait un plan du quartier ,répondu à toutes nos questions ,nous a fait partager un moment de sa vie. La description de l'appartement et conforme à ce que nous avons trouvé sur place. Le logement est fonctionnel et plutôt bien placé dans un quartier sympathique

If you are looking for a nice quite part of Tokyo that is still in the heart of the city then look no further then Asakusa. The apartment we rented was a 5 minute bus ride from the shopping and temple area and other major attractions including the Tokyo Sky Tree. Tony was a great help and offered to drive us to the station and give us directions to the local attractions. The apartment was very clean and had enough room to comfortably sleep 4 people. A new grocery store just opened up less then a block away which comes in handy when you are looking for a place to buy groceries or alcohol. If you are looking for places to buy Japanese chief knifes and other cooking goods / souvenirs then take a quick bus ride and then walk to Kappabashi!
Tony is the best host! Kind, courteous, helpful. A stand-up guy.

The apartment is very nice and comfortable, perfect facilities, very close to asakusa and sky tree. Hirano san is very kind, he picked us up to his apartment when we came to tokyo, he also drove us to the metro station when we check out and going to the airport.

Une arrivée à Tokyo simple et au delà des attendus !! Mr Hirano est venu nous chercher à la station de métro Asakusa (direct depuis Haneda...). Un service très appréciable compte tenu des bagages. Très agréablement accueillis, nous avons eu droit à un petit tour dans Tokyo, pour le repérage des grands quartiers. L'appartement est agréablement situé, au calme à une dizaine de minute de la station de métro Asakusa et du Senso Ji. Il est parfaitement fidèle à la description et dispose de nombreux équipements et de produits cosmétiques. A proximité de restaurants. Nous avons été enchantés.
Merci à Mr Hirano

My husband, Jun, and I remember Japan from about 25 years ago and we have been looking forward to this visit back to Tokyo. Not only was this trip to Japan a first for our two college-aged children, Kato & Kyra, this was the first time that our family made use of the Air BnB service.

After the initial trepidation on which place to choose, I am glad that we had the privilege of ending up in Tony Hirano’s place: a definite home away from home.

Tony-san and his wife Kazue were very welcoming. An added bonus was meeting his daughter-in-law, who happens to be from our own country, too. Her daughters were a delight to meet. Overall, our choice to veer away from usual hotel accommodations was one of the best decisions that made our Tokyo trip extremely worthwhile. There was a lot of walking to do, places to visit, food to taste…. time went by quickly. It would have been a nice option to maybe bike to and from the apartment, too. Watching the local people go about their daily routine on bikes was an added treat.

On behalf of my children and myself, my husband sums up our entire experience with Tony Hirano and family very aptly with this:

If you are looking for a semblance of old Tokyo - look no further. The description of the flat of Tony-san is spot on. His place was clean and had all the basic amenities. There is a grocery approximately one to two minutes walk just around the corner. The bus stops are around three to five minutes’ walk away in one direction. The Asakusa train station is in the other direction, around a 15 -20 minute leisurely stroll from Tony-san’s place. The walk will become something to appreciate as you are slowly surrounded by all the best restaurants to suit your budget, small and large stores to explore, and find yourself just a stone's throw away from the Sensõ-ji Temple with side streets resembling days gone by.

But, the main reason why I would not hesitate to recommend this BnB is our hosts, the Hirano family! Tony-san and his wife Kazue, his daughter-in-law Kat, his two grandchildren Sawako and Yoko were the perfect hosts! From picking us up at the train station, advise on the best mode & route to take to explore Tokyo, lunch and dinner with his family, and so on and so forth...I guarantee your stay will be more than enjoyable.

Kampai! Looking forward to visit again...
Thanks for the wonderful hospitality and the wonderful memories, Tony-san and family!
= From Jun, Susan, Kato & Kyra

We loved this appartment and is localisation in an "autentic" Tokyo area, popular, with a lot of restaurants. It was simple and just wath we wanted. Hirano was very kind and helpful with us. Thanks for this good first experience in Japan !
Sophie and Clement

Hirano San is an excellent host. He is not a simple apartment owner but a japanese citizen interested in introduce Tokyo to your guests and he is really worried about our well being.
I think that Hirano San represent the spirit of airbnb proposal and if one day we come back to Tokyo certainly we'll knock in Hirano san's door.

Paulo

Logement idéalement situé dans le charmant quartier d'Asakusa,spacieux,trés bien équipé.
Tony est un hôte exceptionnel,il fut disponible et attentionné.
Une adresse précieuse!!!!
住宿环境好。房东很热心。还跟我们讲了很多好的商场。

We are truly happy that we chose to book our accommodation with Hirano san who really contributed to making our stay in Tokyo unforgettable! Hirano san is very friendly, easy to communicate with and helpful in every way. We also felt that he was genuinely committed to making our stay as easy and rich as possible. The apartment was clean and comfortable and we felt at home right away. The area is quiet, the communications are very good and there are several 24/7 convenient stores close by. In short a perfect starting point for discovering Tokyo. We would not hesitate to recommend this listing and especially Hirano san as a host!

Many thanks to Tony and his family for all the hospitality.

/John, Robin and Chieko.

我们是一家3人去的，提前1个月预定的房间，房东无微不至的解答。我们非常喜欢浅草的生活环境，便利中不乏喧嚣，这里的房间非常适合居住，我会推荐给我的每一位想去东京旅行的朋友，下次去日本，我还会选择这里。
To irano san: うちの子がまた行きたいので、来年も一緒に飲みましょう。

Thanks for all! Especially for you patient.
Hirano San picked us at the bus stop and show us what's near on the way to the apartment. He gave us many tips and he was always there when we have questions!
The apartment was great. enough space for 4 people.
Thanks!
Un accueil absolument fantastique. Des hôtes soucieux de notre bien être et très chaleureux. Merci encore!!! Le quartier est très sympa.

Appartement très bien situé. Le propriétaire a eu la gentillesse de venir nous chercher à la station de métro la plus proche. L'appartement est situé dans un de droit calme. Par contre ce dernier est mal tenu et pas très propre. La literie sentait mauvais. Dommage car sinon le lieu pourrait être agréable.

Hirano-san has been extremely helpful and extremely kind since the beginning. We've gone out a few times to share a meal together as well, and he has invited me to drinks although I already had plans. He was very prompt and helpful when first going to his place, as well as throughout the stay, and also helped me find the bus stop when I needed to go to the airport.

He's a very nice guy. The room has a tatami mat, which I love. The internet (maybe the router?) is not quite fast enough for video. You just have to let it sit and load for a while before watching. Other than that, everything is great and has exceeded my expectations. Highly recommended!

The apartment was on the top floor of an building, it was good for 3-4 adults. Was good to have a separate toilet and shower room, kitchen was equipped with gas stove (we did not cook), microwaveoven, kettle and fridge. A washing machine was ready for use. If you have heavy luggage bag, it will be a challenge to bring up to 4th floor, however, Hirano-san was very helpful to bring mine up. Thanks! Local store -Tobu /supermarket was just 2 mins  away, and Lawson convenient store was 3 mins walk away. Train to city was 15 mins walk away, Asakusa Senso-ji was 10mins walk away. Train to Shibuya takes abt 20mins. Wifi connection was stable.

Soggiornare nell'appartamento messo a disposizione da Hirano-san è stata una bellissima esperienza. Hirano-san e sua moglie ci hanno accolto con molto calore, prodigandosi per rendere speciale la nostra permanenza ad Asakusa. Ci hanno fornito molti utilissimi consigli, sono stati presenti e disponibili per tutto il soggiorno e ci hanno addirittura invitato a casa loro per una cena tra amici. Il giorno della partenza, inoltre, ci ha portato direttamente alla stazione della metropolitana più vicina, con la loro auto. L'appartamento è un bilocale, con una stanza tradizionale a tatami: perfetto per la nostra famiglia (viaggiamo in tre) e dotato di tutto ciò di cui avevamo bisogno. La scheda di presentazione e le foto corrispondono a tutto quello che abbiamo trovato sul sito. Il quartiere è tranquillissimo, si può girare serenamente a qualsiasi ora del giorno e della notte. Per una settimana abbiamo potuto vivere come la gente del posto. Per raggiungere le altre zone di Tokyo, si può prendere l'autobus 42 (la fermata è a un centinaio di metri dalla casa), che in una ventina di minuti porta alla stazione di Tokyo.  La metropolitana non è vicinissima, ma si può raggiungere con un quarto d'ora di camminata, passando attraverso lo spettacolare Senso-ji. Nei dintorni ci sono diversi supermercati (consigliamo il Tobu store), due bagni pubblici (noi abbiamo provato con soddisfazione entrambi) e numerosi ristorantini. Affidatevi all'host per i consigli! Assolutamente promosso a pieni voti.

Petit appartement très agréable et fonctionnel. Placé dans un quartier  où il y a plein de choses à faire (temples, magasins, restaurants). Hirano-san s'est très bien occupé de nous. Pour les utilisateurs du JR Pass, un peu loin de la ligne Yamanote.

Asakusa is a great base for your Tokyo trip - easy access to both airports and well connected by Metro to all the major sights. Senso-ji, Kappabashi and Minowa or Asakusa Metro stations are all walkable in under 20 min. We enjoyed coming home to a charming, quiet neighbourhood and getting a taste of old Tokyo after long days exploring the hectic, modern parts of the city. Tony's place is clean, bright, simple and the perfect size for a couple, with all necessary amenities. There's a great view of Skytree from the balcony. Tony and his family were warm, kind and generous hosts - we will definitely choose to stay here again next time we visit Tokyo; we're already making plans!

Tony, is a terrific host who loves people, is friendly and completely welcoming. He is a wonderful person. We will remember the experience with him always. Tony is the best thing about staying at his home. Apart from that, the apartment was cozy and clean, and a short walk from Asakusa Station, and the Asakusa temple and shopping grounds. Several food options and convenience stores within one minute's walk. A bit further, and the options multiply 100 fold. A great base to explore Tokyo. Highly recommended.

ใจดี และเป็นกันเองมากครับ แนะนำเลย ห้องกว้างและสะอาด อยู่กันสามสี่คนสบายเลย ที่พักต้องนั่งรถเมล์ไปห้าสถานีจากสถานี asakusa ครับ แต่สะดวกอยู่ครับ รถเมล์วิ่งบ่อยมาก Kindly,friendly host. He has a lot of useful informations. Clean and large room. It's comfortable room for 3-4 people. Mansion is far 5 bus stops from asakusa station but bus is very frequenly so it is ok. Much recommend.

Tony is a wonderful host. He was very good at communicating directions, checked up on us throughout our stay, and suggested places for us to go. He picked us up and dropped us off at Asakusa Station upon arrival and departure, which was very much appreciated.
Tony's place is a short walk to Asakusa Station, and Senjoji Temple. It is located in a quiet neighbourhood, which we welcomed after long days in the hustle and bustle of Tokyo.
Overall, a great stay, and would definitely stay here again.
I loved the quiet and family friendly area. The apartment is clean and tidy and comfortable. And Hirano San is the best host ever!

Thank you very much for Tony's all arrangement. Day 1 we arrived Tokyo you pick me up at the train station, the room is clean and tidy as the picture on airbnb. Day 2 we have a chance to have a dinner with you and your wife at japanese restaurant. Day 4 you invite us to have a dinner at your home, my daughter play with your 2 granddaughter, the yummy foods & wine both your wife made, there are a lot of laugh, fun around these 3 hours. Let us know more about Japanese culture. Last day you deliver us to the train station.  All the above make us very miss both of you and Tokyo.  We must book your room if we travel to Tokyo again in the future because you are a very very nice guy.  Hope you and your family are healthy and happiness everyday.

We had a great stay in Tokyo with Hirano San. The accommodation was perfect for us the flat was large for Tokyo and well stocked with everything we needed. Hirano San was available to help with any questions as he is located on site.

Staying at Hirano Sans top floor apartment gives a great view of residential Asakusa. Authentic Japanese neighbourhood with an insight into the past, Asakusa is a wonderful place to start from with temples locally an a 15 minute walk to Asakusa subway with local busses 2 minutes away.
We enjoyed walking the streets and seeing the locals getting about their lives and talking photos of the local buildings.
I suggest buying a Tokyo wide train pass for about 10,000 yen which gives access to the JE East lines and Shinkansen (bullet train). With this pass we went to MtFuji as well as other locations Tokyo wide.
I highly recommend You stay here if you want an authentic taste of Japanese culture and after our 2nd stay now, Tony is a fine man whose English is very good, he and his wife helped us enjoy our stay.

L' appartamento, di piccole dimensioni, ha una camera da letto in tipico stile giapponese. È situato in un piccola palazzina di poche unità. Il quartiere è residenziale, ma a 10 minuti a piedi si possono trovare locali dove pranzare e cenare. La stazione della metropolitana è a 20 minuti. Vicinissimo a casa c'è un piccolo supermercato rifornitissimo se decidete di non uscire per mangiare. Hirano è stato molto disponibile nell'accoglierci e nel fornirci assistenza.

Hirano Family is best option that you can choose. Really you will not find some family similar in all Japan, believe me. The accommodation is comfortable and centrally located. Two minutes from the bus and 10 minutes from the subway. The family is always willing to help you if you need it.

A good-sized apartment in the quiet back streets of Asakusa. It was just around the corner from bus stops and three stops away from the nearest train station. Hirano San was very helpful and kept in touch with us in person and via Messenger if we needed any advice.
We would recommend his place to anyone who can hoist their bags up and down the stairs! Obviously, you should not stay there if the accessibility would be an issue.

Our time at Tony's place was great! The house is very spacious for a Japanese apartment, and even though the furniture is a bit old, everything feels really comfortable. The neighborhood is really calm and cosy, with all the shops and transport options nearby. There is a grocery store one block away from the apartment and is really good. There you'll find awesome fruit, fish and meat and great and cheap home made traditional japanese food like sushi, tempura, etc. The only 2 less positive things to mention about the house is that the wi-fi was not as good as we would have expected. You can only have 1 device connected to have a normal speed, and the level of cleaning when we arrived was not at our desired standard. We had to some cleaning ourselves.
Tony was the best landlord, always willing to help and make your stay as good as possible. He even took as to the train station for free on our check out. Thank you very much Tony, we will definitly rent again if we go back to Tokyo!!

We stayed here for a week while we explored Tokyo and it was a good home base for the three of us; it's small but clean and in quiet neighborhood away from the craziness of Tokyo.  It's close to several local bus routes that took us into Asakusa or to the Minowa Station, and there are many small restaurants and a grocery store nearby, as well as the Senso-ji Temple and Tokyo SkyTree so we could do some sightseeing within minutes of leaving the apartment.

Tony was a great host! He picked us up from the subway station and gave us a tour of the neighbourhood. The apartment was clean and quiet, with everything we could have asked for. The bedroom had beautiful tatami mats and futons for sleeping. Thank you for having us!

The apartment and area were surprisingly quiet, particularly outside office hours. If you're not in a hurry or carrying luggage, it's really worth walking to the train station via various routes to see this district. The apartment had everything we needed and we were easily able to cook and get a good sleep.

Tony is the best!
Asakusa feels like a secret tip in Tokyo. If you forget the Senso-ji temple and the Sky Tree (both crowded & in a way boring), and just walk down the streets of the neighborhood, you will be able to explore a lot of awesome people, cafes & sights.
We had a short, but great time with Tony and we will come back very soon :)
If you have the pleasure to meet Tony, go ask him for location tips in Asakusa & follow them. You won't be disappointed, I promise!
PS: Please don't rent this apartement, so we can book it when we travel to Tokyo =D.

Hirano est venu nous chercher à la station de métro comme convenu à notre arrivée, il nous a également gentiment accompagné à la gare le jour de notre départ, très tôt, avant le démarrage des transports en commun.
Salle de bains à la japonaise, lits au sol, supérette juste à côté.

Nice small apartment which is located at a very good location (near Sensoji Temple & Skytree).
Tony (Hirano San) is very helpful & considerate host.
The flexible check in time is an added advantage as most bnb check in was after 4pm, & since our arrival was at 7.20am.

Very happy in Hirano's flat.
I will enjoy to come again

Great place for great price.
Very quiet sleep in calm neighborhoods.
Cozy little apartment under the roof.
Perfect for our family of 4.
Supermarket/playground/ restaurants/hot baths around the corner.
Kaminarimon, Supersento and shopping arcade in walking distance.
Host is most hospitable, he was so kind to us, early check in and drove us from/to station with heavy luggage. Many advices about where to go and opening hours.
Great experience!

Tonys home is incredibly comfortable and clean. It also has a fantastic view of Tokyo skytree and a very easy to use kitchen space. His apartment is located very concurrently to ann awesome grocery shop and a short distance to trains. Tony is also a wonderfully generous host and very knowledgeable. He took us to a very economical sushi and sumo training restaurant and the food was incredibly delicious. He ids also very kind and understanding, eeasy to get a hold of and very friendly. Hee certainly has exceeded the standards of what it means to be an Airbnb host and we plan to utilize his space and hospitality in the future.
Logement petit mais fonctionnel. Il y a tout ce qu' il faut. Compter 15 min à pied de la station de métro. Tony est tout un personnage !

It was our first time in Japan. Staying at Tony's flat gave us an opportunity toget toknow the old-fashioned Tokyo.
The flat is very nice and comfortable, well equipped; located a few minutes from a bus stop and about 15-20 minutes to Asakusa Station.
I can't find words good enough to describe Tony's hospitality and openness.
To live in Asakusa those few days was a really fantastic expierence. To have Tony as a host is the best thing that could happen to us.
I really appreciate the time w spent together.
Than You Tony and Kazue for everything.

Tony was a very helpful and attentive host! It is more difficult to get to this location using the JR rail passes, but using other public transportation is very easy and accessible. The main attractions in Asakusa were also very easy to walk to from this location! Great stay overall!

Tonys flat is really nice. We were travelling with two children and we had enough space. There is enough stuff for cooking and a supermarket just around the corner. Tony and his wife were very friendly. Tony even drove us around, we went for lunch together, he showed us a nice park and he drove us to the train station. He is a very good host! We would definitely return.

Mr. Hirano is a perfect host. He did his best for help us, so we can only say thank to him.
The apartment is in central area, (btw difficult find a real center in Tokyo). It is clean, comfortable with everything need and after "a day walk" find such cozy place was very helpful.
The grocery store, a minute walk, is good and offer everything includes different choice of set dinner.
The area is quite, very quite, the advantage is that you can have good rest (and we appreciated it), the downside is that you must walk for a while before find some restaurant.
Definetly was a good choice for us.
Super fast pick up upon arrival at the train station. Very nice and sweet place. Will stay again when I’m  Tokyo. Thanks from the states.

L'appartement est très agréable et ensoleillé, avec vue sur la Skytree depuis son petit balcon. A quelques minutes à pied des temples d'Asakusa. Un peu loin du métro, mais l’arrêt de bus est juste à côté.
Tony a été très accueillant et toujours disponible pour répondre à nos questions. Il est venu nous chercher en voiture et nous a également amenés à la gare le jour du départ.

비록 체크인에 문제가있었으나 큰문제는아니였어요 숙소를예약했는데 너무나 맘에들었고 편안한 일본가정집을 체험하기에 더할나위없이 좋았네요  저희는 임산부가있어 역까지 택시타고다녔어요 기본요금나오구요 그게아님 주변구경하면서 걷기에 좋아요 센소지구경하기엔 거리가 도보거리여서 너무 편했어요 주인분은 너무나 친절하고 다정해요 설명도 잘해주시고 편안히 숙소쉴수있도록 배려해주셔서 너무 감사했어요 다음에도 이숙소에 찾아오고싶어요  thank you hirano san!!!

Tony is an amazing host. From even before the trip began he was communicating to make sure our stay was fantastic. The apartment and neighborhood felt like home after just a few days and I felt like I had made a friend in Tony. Couldn’t recommend him enough.
闹中取静，社区非常安静，离浅草商圈步行15分钟，到晴空塔步行20分钟，到浅草站步行15分钟，体验真日本的最佳选择。

We had a wonderful and comfortable stay.  The apartment had all the necessary amenities for my family of four.  The location was  very convenient.  It was walking distance to a local super market, convenient stores, restaurants, shops, and parks.  It was a 15-20 minute walk to Asakusa subway station or a few minutes away from the bus stop.  Tony and his wife were gracious and kind hosts.  Tony was communicative and helpful.  He picked us up and dropped us off at the subway station, an amazing service he provides to make traveling easy for the family.  A big thank you to Tony and his wife for helping us make our trip a memorable and fun experience.  I definitely recommend his place and would love to stay there again on our next visit to Tokyo.

Tonys herzliche Gastfreundschaft und seine hilfreichen Infos machen den Aufenthalt unkompliziert. Die Unterkunft ist groß genug für drei Leute, die Lage in Asakusa ist ruhig und ein wenig abseits von Tokios Gewimmel. Dank der nahen U-Bahn kann man Tokio dennoch gut erkunden. Die berühmte Tempelanlage ist zu Fuß easy erreichbar, es gibt etliche konbinis in der Nähe sowie andere Einkaufsmöglichkeiten. Was will man mehr! Uns hat’s hier gut gefallen!

We had a wonderful time at Tony's apartment  in the beautiful district Asakusa. The place is situated in a quiet neighbourhood, a supermarket just around the next corner and restaurants in walking distance. If the weather is fine you can walk to Asakusa station in about 20 minutes or just take the bus which brings you there in 10 min. The apartement is well equipped will all kinds of stuff for the kitchen, a washing machine easy to handle and an air conditioner in the bed room. The communication with Tony was really smooth, he always responded immediately, gave us useful tips and recommendations (such as a great Ramen place nearby) and was regularly checking on our wellbeing. Tony is a very kind host and even insisted on giving us money for a taxi since he could not pick us up at the metro station at the day if our arrival, for which we are really grateful. We would absolutely recommend to stay at Tony's place during a trip to Tokyo and wish him all the best.
Logement dans un quartier calme , propre , tout à fait conforme au descriptif .
잘 놀다갑니다
A very friendly and very quiet place, in a peaceful area. Perfect accommodation  for 3 persons.
房东人很热情，给了我们很多建议。房子旁边有个东武超市，东西很便宜。房子稍显有点旧，但还是挺干净的。房间内还能看到天空树。我们坐地铁都是跑到南千柱地铁站去坐，10分钟的路程吧。
Wonderful host!  Although we got lost trying to find the place, Hirano San was kind enough to wait outside till we got there. Very spacious apartment compared to other places in Japan

This is our second time to travel in Tokyo. Unlike the first time we stayed in an expensive hotel with a small room, this time we tried airbnb,hoping to experience the real Japanese life, staying in a Japanese house and making friends with Japanese people. We are lucky to find Tony’s apartment which is located in a safe and quiet district with a park, a supermarket, and convenience stores nearby. The apartment is clean and very quiet at night. You can definitely have a good night sleep. Cooking in the apartment is allowed. There is also a vacuum cleaner and a washing machine for us to use. With a family of five people, two adults and three children, the apartment is quite spacious for us. But the best thing to stay here is our host,Tony, who tries his best to make ourselves home.  Tony knows a lot and he carries on good conversations. Our host is a good advisor telling us where to dine, to visit and to have fun and he is also a generous kind man offering nice good candy, treats and toys for my kids.(Kids are really happy with the surprise!!) When the weather was really hot at noon, Tony cared about my three young kids and he kindly drove us to the subway and the place where we were going.(Great Big Thanks,Tony.) With Tony’s company, our trip is such a wonderful unforgettable experience!!
房东是一位非常热情的人，与我们交流颇多，非常周到，周边生活设施齐全，离浅草也只有几站的距离，房间总体干净，只有部分角落有灰尘，房龄较大，四楼没有电梯，房东非常负责任，也很好沟通，性价比很高，还会再来入住。
Hirano-san's place is very comfortable and well maintained. The host is very helpful and friendly. I highly recommend his place for visiting Tokyo.

We couldn’t recommend Tony’s hospitality enough. From arriving to Tokyo, where he came to pick us up, to our last day where he offered to drop us directly to the station, the service provided was nothing but great. Tony’s advices proved to be valuable and added an unique local flavour to our trip, and his flat is a friendly place where one feels immediately at ease. Located in Asukasa, with all 24/7 convenience stores nearby, The Cherry Crest is a few minutes walk from the impressive Senso-ji temple, from which you can easily reach Asukasa station. The Asakusa Line will take you to both Narita and Haneda airports, and the Ginza Line goes straight to Shibuya.
If you are looking for a quiet and conveniently located place to stay in Tokyo with a friendly host - This is your place!
Hirano san is a very good host with well known Japanese behavior. Very good and calm place, with necessary for cooking and many shops and restaurants around. I recommend this place.

Tony, thank you for being such a gracious and fantastic host. The apartment was impeccable and had all the facilities we needed. We had an amazing trip and can't wait to come back, we will definitely stay with you again next time we are in Japan, thank you for some amazing memories and lifelong friendships made i would recommend this apartment 100 stars from us!!! :) xx

Hirano san's place is on a quiet street. A pleasant walk along a skinny park nearby will take you to the Sumida river, which is again good for walking or a morning run. Plenty of amenities nearby, like convenience stores, small restaurants, a Denny's, an excellent sushi restaurant and a public bath. Hirano san is always helpful and a good English speaker. There are three subway stations, but all are at least a kilometer away. The walk is pleasant though, if you chose the right route. Anyway, Tony is so accommodating he  will pick you up or drop you off.  The Asakusa Station will get you to Narita or Haneda easily. The apartment is spacious by Tokyo standards, has kitchen and all other western-style amenities. Even an iron and ironing board! Quiet street but there was building going on in Sept-Oct 2018 (though it did not start up until 7am or so). If I say any more good things about Hirano san's place I won't be able to find room for myself next year!
Excellent place, totally recommend it!

家具齊全，可以煮食
戶主非常親切，介紹屋附近地點
三人入住，地方大小相對合適
唯獨沒有

The place is excellent. Its location at a very quiet neighborhood made it special. Kind neighbors, too. The kindness, generosity and thoughtfulness of Tony made it even better.
The proximity to the Sensoji temple and transport stations of the place, plus places to eat, makes it a great location.
One of the best vacations my family and I ever had.

Hirano San is a really great host. This was our first visit to Japan, and he was the first person we met. Thank him for making a very good impression on us about the Japanese. Before our arrival, he gave us very clear instructions about the venue. He personally met us near the subway and brought to his home. The apartment was clean and big enough for me and my son. He explained to us how to use all home appliances. He also explained to us how to use the bus, train, etc. This was very helpful. Indeed, the location is perfect. Bus 2 minutes on foot and in 7 minutes you can reach the subway. 15 minutes walk to the historic center of Asakusa. To the subway a maximum of 20 minutes on foot. Once we spent several hours together in the Asakusa area. It was great :) On the last day, he personally took us to the bus station. It was a straight line to Tokyo Station. Hirano-san, thanks again for your hospitality (ありがとうございます). Next time we will definitely stay at your home :) I highly recommend.
Tony was a warm and welcoming host. It was a pleasure to stay in his apartment, which was centrally located while still being quiet and peaceful. We hope to be back soon. Thank you Tony!

Hirano-san was a great host.  It was very helpful to be picked up from the train station on arrival. The apartment has all the amenities including a washing machine, so we could do laundry during our 5 day stay. There is a bus stop within a minutes walk, and the bus takes you to the Asakusa train station. Sensoji temple is also close by. All in all,  it was a convenient place to stay for sight seeing in Tokyo.

Amazing place to stay Tony is super friendly and helpful! Location is within short walking distance to a beautiful temple, a 15min walk to a subway that will take you straight to akihabara! ( other transit is much closer)

My partner and I enjoyed a wonderful stay at Hirano sans homely apartment in historic Asakusa - Hirano went above and beyond as a thoughtful, knowledgeable and caring host from the very first contact. Apartment description is accurate - We wish we could stay longer ! Until next time - Cannot recommend enough !

Overall a reasonable experience and Hirano-san himself is exceptionally helpful and available. The accommodations are not as new and clean as some others, but the value of this place comes from interaction with Hirano-san himself. The pick up service and his help with navigation was very helpful. On the other hand, we misunderstood that the apartment had a clothes dryer which was difficult for us to manage with limited clothing.
Great flat, large and quiet, with a beautiful view on the Tokyo Skytree. Tony is very kind and helpfull. We enjoy and highly recomand this place.

An amazing apartment with a proper Japanese style bedroom. The futons were very comfortable. Everything was really clean and easy to use. The area has loads of restaurants, convenience store and a supermarket but is also really quiet at night. You get a great view of the skytree on the balcony.
Hirano San was extremely accommodating, he picked us up at the station when we arrived and dropped us off when we checked out. Hirano San's knowledge of the area is extensive and he can provide lots of tips for where to go/what to do.
The apartment is within easy walking distance of Asakusa Station or you can catch a bus from closer to the apartment. The Asakusa line itself is really good for getting around as it appears to not be a commuter line so you tend to not be squished onto extremely crowded trains as often. You can also get to most places without needing to change trains more than once. Asakusa itself is a great place and the walk back from the station at night is a great opportunity to see Sensoji illuminated.
We would definitely stay here again and recommend the apartment very highly.

Our stay at Tony s place was amazing !!!! We really appreciated  staying in this flat , perfect for our family . Everything you need to cook ,  clean, relax is there . The flat is very well located with bus station, supermarket, many restaurants ( Tony has good adress !!! ) . Nice view from the little balcony ! And the best part was sharing time with our host because Tony is an amazing and a real  character ! Thank you for this stay in Tokyo :) because with you it was a great experience ! So just go to Tony s place and enjoy !

Tonys Wohnung ist der perfekte Ort, um in Tokio anzukommen und die Gegend zu erkunden. Unsere kleine Familie (2 Kids, 5+8 Jahre) hat sich sehr wohl gefühlt und war dankbar gleich an der Bahnstation empfangen worden zu sein. Die Tipps und Empfehlungen von Tony (z. B. eine leckere Nudelbar um die Ecke) waren stets hilfreich. Asakusa ist ein interessantes Viertel und ein guter Ausgangsort, um weitere Viertel zu erkunden. In Laufweite (auch für Kids) sind diverse Busse leicht erreichbar. Die Wohnung ist ausreichend groß und gut ausgestattet - mit tollem Blick auf den Tokyo Skytree. Sehr zu emfpehlen!

From Haneda Airport to Tony's place is very easy. Just take Asakusa route to A18 and Exit at A2b. No transfers! A good half an hour or less travel.
Tony picked us up in 5 minute's.
There are many convenience stores nearby and a Denny's restaurant.
It is also a 10 to 12 minute walk to The Asakusa Temple tourist attraction.
Tony's place has tatami sleeping area which is a cool experience for our kids. The apartment is very cool at night and it is located at a very quiet neighborhood.
We don't have to line up for Ikinari steak, a 5minute wait at Ichiran.
There is a good Unagi restaurant near Asakusa Station also.
Tony is very helpful, ask anything and he can show you directions and if time permits he will accompany you there too. He went out of his way to drive us to subway station on our flight back home.
Love to go back there.
Tony est un hôte charmant. L'appartement est très bien situé , il est propre mais pour 4 , c'est très intime 😀
Top!

Appartement un peu vieillot mais conforme au photos de l’annonce. Une petite erreur dans la description de l’appartement, il n’y a pas de jacuzzi.
L’appartement est assez loin des métros mais le bus passe au bout de la rue.
Hirano est un hôte très serviable, ce qui peut être profitable quand le séjour n’est pas tout à fait préparé. Dans notre cas, la barrière de la langue (notre petit niveau d’anglais n’est pas suffisant) et le fait que notre voyage était très préparé ont plutôt entraîné des incompréhensions.
Located in Higashi Asakusa, minutes walk to Dennys and Lawson, Sensoji Temple, Asakusa.

We could not have asked for a better host or place to stay in Tokyo. The apartment has a great view of the Tokyo Skytree, and is in an excellent, quiet location near many ammenties. There's a really great sushi place and gyoza place a short walk away which is a must to visit! The apartment itself has everything we needed, including maps and helpful guides on how to use some of the appliances. We will definitely stay again the next time we are in Tokyo!

Tony-san's place is great. Asakusa ended up being our favorite area of Tokyo because it felt so peaceful and safe. The only bad part about this location, is that it is about a 20 min walk to the Asakusa station, which got a little tiresome after a few days. The hospitality is great, and it is very handy that Tony-san lives so close to the Airbnb! Would definitely recommend as a good Tokyo option!

We had a great stay here with our family of four. Tony was an excellent host, picking us up and dropping us off at the train station and providing lots of recommendations. The house was comfortable and had everything we needed, with a supermarket very close by. It was a bit of walk to the nearest subway station, but found a nice route along the river with some good playgrounds for the kids!  Thanks Tony!

If you are coming to Tokyo staying here is a must! The apartment is very comfortable and much more roomy than expected, there is also a heap of amenities and it is easy to cook a meal. Additionally, the area is great there are lawsons, 7,11, a supermarket and a lot of great restaurants to eat at which Tony will happily suggest to you, most of which are within walking distance. As for getting around the rest of Tokyo it is not difficult. The area of Asakusa (not the shrine of course!) itself is lovely and in winter is more filled with locals than tourists which was a bonus for us. Additionally to get to the closest station you can take a nice walk along Sumida river (we even got luck with some plum blossoms) or catch a bus down. Lastly, our host, tony was the most caring and attentive Airbnb host I’ve ever stayed with. On our first day we went on a nice sushi lunch and bonsai garden tour which was spectacular. Furthermore before we left to Mount Fuji there was bad snow and road conditions. Tony was nice enough to keep us updated on all the TV reports. All in all this place and the host made our first stay in Tokyo amazing, until next time :)

Hospedaje totalmente recomendado! Hirano San es un gran anfitrión!! Él te da muchos consejos a dónde puedes ir a comer o a comprar cosas y siempre se puede tener una muy agradable conversación con él.

My only regret is that we were unable to spend more time with Tony as we already had so much planned to do. The few times we did, is was always memorable. A perfect place to spend a few weeks in Japan. He was responsive and gracious and friendly above and beyond.
His place is a nice little traditional apartment. Comfortable futons, a small bath with shower, and a mini kitchen, it had a surprising amount of space to put our stuff down and a balcony with a view of the the Tokyo Skytree. Clean, and with a heater that starts working the moment you turn it on for the chillier spring nights.
The neighbourhood is peaceful, an easy (if slightly lengthy) walk to both Minowa and Asakusa Stations, and has a Denny's and a Lawson a minute away. And a full supermarket a little further.
Should I return, this place will be top of places to look into staying.

The apartment is comfortable and quiet. We liked the beautiful Japanese-style tatami mats and futons for sleeping. The gas cooking is very practical. We would look forward to staying again and highly recommend it.
Tony went above and beyond to meet us at the Higashi-Asakusa bus stop nearby and provided a warm friendly welcome, guidance, and local tips. On departure, he checked the times and dropped us at the Asakusa station direct to Haneda Airport. We very much appreciate the time flexibility on checkout. Communication was caring, attentive, and responsive – best host ever!
The location is walkable to Asakusa attractions including Sensō-ji Buddhist temple in 15 mins and the Ueno Park area in 40 minutes. Tobu Store, close by, is a larger supermarket. Asakusa subway station is approx. 20 mins walk. Tokyo Cruise is just beside the subway. Asakusa Hoppi, Denboin Street have many shops and restaurants. The guides at the apartment were very useful.

Hiranoさん was very hospitable, him and his family treated us like one of their own. I am truly grateful for their kindness. They were amazingly helpful in recommending areas to visit and eat, even being a tourguide for great spots to find local spots.
I can not praise him and his family enough for their generosity. Thank you Hiranoさん

I loved this part of Tokyo, which felt cozy, yet fairly centrally  located. Hirano-san was particularly helpful with information and suggestions about the surroundings. I wish I could have stayed longer so I could spend more time with him and learn about the local culture. He is a great family man, and kudos to him for helping us during our stay. I will definitely stay again if I ever go back to Tokyo.

Tony was simply the best.
There's no simple way to put it, you just have to come.
He came to pick us up from the metro station with clear instructions, then proceeded to tell us about the area and invited us to a restaurant nearby.
The appartment is quite big (for tokyo) the futon beds in the tatami room are very comfortable (good for 2/3ppl).
The shower is big, and there already was soap/conditioner/shampoo, even toothpaste and plasters.
The area is wonderful, Tokyo is a big city but Asakusa felt more like a village and we quickly felt right at home.
Of course none of this would be the same without Tony. I think of him more as a friend than as our host. We were lucky to have him as our guide during sanja matsuri, he introduced us to a lot of people in the neighborhood and gave us priceless memories.
We'll surely come back to see Tokyo and stay at his apartment again.
Would write more about Tony but Airbnb lets me write only 1000 words.
Love, Lucas and Chanie ♥️

とても親切なオーナーさん、奥様でした。
部屋からスカイツリーが見えて、毎晩、綺麗でした。
また、静かな街で、よく眠れました。スーパーやコンビニ、浅草駅へのバス停も近く便利でした。
街の人も話しかけたり、挨拶して下さったり、アットホームな街でした。
This was a perfect cozy apartment to use as a homebase while exploring Tokyo. Tony was very friendly, and full of good suggestions. He definitely made my stay easier.
房东非常热情，住宿体验很满意。

Hinaro San es el mejor anfitrión de Air BnB que hemos tenido. Muy atento siempre. Nos recogió en la estación, nos invitó a la tradicional fiesta de fuegos artificiales que organizó en la azote del edificio. La casa de estilo japonés es preciosa y al lado de supermercados y del BUS que te lleva a cualquier lugar de Tokyo. Volvermos a alojarnos en este apartamento.

We, a family of three from Berlin, stayed at Tony's apartment for only 6 days. We would have loved to stay there longer because of Tony's hospitality and his nice family.
The apartment felt like home and it was equipped with everything needed for daily life. The location could not have been better; There was a supermarket and many convenience stores only a short walk away. Two bus stations were also nearby so we could we could get to the subway quickly.
Tony gave us an insight into the local Japanese culture of Asakusa by inviting us to a beautiful Summer Festival from the neighborhood.
Tony and his family invited us to a nice Okonomiyaki restaurant for dinner. His family taught us how to prepare the delicious Okonomiyaki. It was a great experience to learn more about the japanese cuisine. We won’t forget this unique night with his nice family.
If you want to stay in a cozy apartment with nice people around you, we highly recommend you go there.
Thank you Tony for being our host!

con la mia famiglia (2 adulti+9anni+12anni) per 5 giorni a Tokyo. 1,2 km da asakusa metro e 1,2km da minowa metro. 100mt da bus. zona sicura e tranquilla. forse un po' piccolo ma spartano l'alloggio con 2 stanze, un bagno e una cucina.

Hirano san is a very kind gentleman, he came to meet us at the Bus station near his Airbnb. This is one of his apartments in his building which is a little old but large enough for my family of three adults.  All the necessary amenities and kitchen and bath are here. 1 minute walk to the supermarket (open 9am-9pm). 1 minute to Denny's for the inexpensive breakfast. Two bus stops, one goes to Asakusa Temple, and the other bus goes to Ueno area and Ikebukuro area. 210 yen for a ride. We spent few days in Asakusa where you can walk around all day, we had so much fun! We bought the one day Metro subway passes for 600 yen ($4) each, unlimited rides for 24 hours. This airbnb is located very convenient location, and yet very quiet in the night. When we got tired walking, we took a cab from Asakusa subway station to airbnb, it cost less than 1000 yen.

Hirano San's place was fantastic. It had everything we needed, it was lovely to have space to move around, great cooling as it was very hot and humid, comfortable and private. We could walk to the temples nearby, a great supermarket only 1min walk away, lots of bus stops nearby as well which will get to everywhere you'd like to go. Hirano San was auch a great host, picked us up from the bus stop and also invited us out for a day trip to Odaiba and a shared family birthday at a nearby steakhouse. We were so lucky to find this great apartment and such a fantastic, kind and friendly host. Thank you Tony, you made our stay very special.

Nous avons passé un très bon séjour chez Tony. L'appartement est très propre et confortable. Le quartier est top car il permet de découvrir des lieux plus authentiques et plusieurs bus sont  à  côté qui vous emmènent partout. Le magasin Tobu est très pratique et juste à côté également. Enfin, Tony est très aidant, sympathique et compréhensif. Nous recommandons fortement !! Merci Tony !

Es war wirklich toll, in Tonys Wohnung zu wohnen. Wir haben uns direkt wohl gefühlt und es hat uns an nix gefehlt. Der japanische Charakter der Wohnung hat uns total gefallen und die Futons waren richtig gemütlich!
Tony war ein toller und zuvorkommender Gastgeber. Er hat uns bei unserer Ankunft vom Bahnhof abgeholt und uns begeistert von der Wohngegend berichtet. Das war an unserem ersten Tag in Japan unglaublich hilfreich.
Die Lage der Wohnung ist toll! Das Wohngebiet ist total ruhig und es fahren mehrere Busse in direkter Umgebung los. Asakusa Station und alle wichtigen Attraktionen von Asakusa sind zu Fuß in 20 min zu erreichen.
In der Gegend gibt es einige kleinere Spielplätze und mehrere Supermärkte.
Had a great time, Hirano is very friendly and takes care to make guests feel welcome and at home.

Tony and his home are number 1!
We love the Asakusa area and we are so glad that we found his place - the best part was meeting Tony and his family! They are so friendly, welcoming and kind!
Even after some mixup on our part, Tony gladly picked us up from the station and brought us to his home which was extremely helpful.
His place is just as the photos show; it is clean and homey and we felt comfortable right away.
There are combinis and a market very nearby which is very convenient; there are a few bus stops as well and it is a very nice ~15 min walk to the Asakusa station / Senso-ji area which we preferred to do a few times.
We will definitely love to stay at Tony’s place again next time we are in Tokyo and we will highly recommend it to our friends and family!
Mahalo, Tony!

Higashi-Asakusa is a nice and quiet neighborhood, located about a 15-minute walk from Senso-ji Temple and the Sumida River. Tony's apartment has a very convenient location with many konbini stores around, an excellent supermarket, and relaxing public baths nearby. The apartment is highly functional, featuring two bedrooms and a separate kitchen. Tony was a great and kind host, and showed us around the neighborhood and Ryougoku and even offered us a delicious lunch. We had a lovely three week stay and will definitely recommend it!

Staying here was like staying at your favorite aunt/uncle's house. Tony looked out for us from the time we landed till we arrived at 9pm. The area is quiet and peaceful, we would open the windows and nap with the cool breeze. Sleeping on traditional comfortable Japanese mats with fluffy blankets put us to sleep every night. We went food shopping at the Tobu and come "home" to cook, or if we wanted something different, Lawson and Denny's with a Michelin star chef is a 1min walk. Able to use all appliances, wifi and enough pots/utensils for a family bigger than the 2 of us. The tub/shower combo was nice, especially after exploring the sumida river which is 6 mins away. The airbnb has a washer, great if you want to pack less or bring home clean clothes. Tony was more than a host, he knows all the places to eat, explore, sightsee, and even gave a great recommendation for a practioner after straining my back. I HIGHLY recommend stay here and would absolutely book again. Tony-Our New Friend!
Great hosts convenient for transport.  Everything you need,  nothing you don't.  Walkable to Senso Ji Temple, restaurants and shops.  Great supermarket 1 min walk. Comfortable beds.
Toni hat sich toll um uns gekümmert. Seine Tipps sehr hilfreich. Der Ausflug mit ihm hat Spaß gemacht. Jederzeit wieder!

C'est sûrement le meilleur Airbnb qui m'ait jamais accueilli. Tony est un hôte très accueillant, toujours à disposition. Son Airbnb est extrêmement propre avec tous les produits et ustensiles nécessaires. Les futons sont incroyablement confortables et le quartier est très calme. La ligne Asakusa et GINZA sont très proches grâce au bus qui se trouve à 2 minutes de l'Airbnb. C'est un très bon Airbnb avec un excellent rapport qualité-prix et il vaut absolument le détour.

A wonderful host. Unlike most AIRBnBs, where the host is never seen, Hirano San lives in the same building and is available for help in any way. Very friendly and approachable.
The location was a little farther from Asakusa Station than I had guessed, but it can be reached via an easy bus ride, or a 15 minute walk.

Hirano San is very friendly and welcoming. It was easy to find his place and it was the perfect size for our family. Plenty of room. We became very familiar with the local buses to get around. They were prompt and allowed us access to the whole city. A/C worked great and kept us cool in the warm weather.  Would definitely stay again when exploring Tokyo and the Asakusa region.

Nous avons passé un très bon séjour chez Toni. Il était présent pour nous guider à notre arrivée et nous a conseiller sur les bons restaurants et activités à faire.
Calme et bien équipé, le logement était parfait pour nous.
Une bonne adresse à retenir pour son emplacement et la gentillesse de Toni.
Schöne, ruhige Unterkunft in entspanntem  Wohnquartier. Für 4 Erwachsene ausreichend Platz und mit allem wss man braucht ausgestattet. Toni ist sehr nett und zuvorkommend. Herzlichen Dank

Fue como estar en casa. Tenía  todo lo necesario para pasar unos días. Lo malo es que hizo mucho calor en Tokyo esos días y solo había aire acondicionado en la habitación. Bien comunicado por autobús pero un poco lejos del metro, y nosotros sacamos una tarjeta de transporte para el metro. Hirano nos dio mucha información del barrio e incluso una tienda de cerámica de un amigo en Kyoto. No tuvimos tiempo de hacer alguna de los planes que nos sugirió y es que hay mucho que hacer en Tokyo. Lo recomiendo para vivir el auténtico Tokyo.

Nous étions très bien accueillis par Tony, qui parle un excellent anglais et qui nous a donné beaucoup de conseils utiles. Son appartement est bien equipé et les futons étaient très confortables. Il est dans un quartier très calme et agréable mais très proche des transports et principaux attractions d'Asakusa qui est un très bon point de départ pour partir à la découverte de Tokyo.

Merci pour votre aide et vos conseil.
Nous recommandons le logement !!!!!
Hôte très accueil, très serviable.
Le logement est facilement accessible en transport en commun et bien situé.
A l’occasion de notre prochain voyage à Tokyo, nous reprenons ce logement.
Encore un grand merci 😊

Tony-San was the bomb! Totally easy-going and super-friendly. He spoke English and Japanese and gave us the down-lo on all things Tokyo. He directed us to a really cool festival Otorisama right in our neighborhood. My nephew (fluent in Japanese) and I not out to dinner with him and his lovely wife. It was a hilarious night with brilliant conversation. He even dialed me in to some cool restaurants back home in Texas. (He knows a frequent traveler who stays with him that owns an authentic Taiwanese cuisine restaurant—very cool.)
The apartment was super convenient with easy to follow instructions. It could easily accommodate a family of four. I had the main bedroom to myself and stretched out over the 2-tatami bed, which was a luxury as most budget accommodations are NARROW. So, very glad to get spoiled for the last leg of our journey in Japan.
Located in Asakusa, the apt is within easy striking distance to a number of must-go destinations. Highly recommended; will stay with him again!
Muy agradable en todos los sentidos, la mejor acomodación en la que he estado en japon, no solo pro la ubicacion, sino por lo hogareño que resulto la vivienda, fue como estar en casa

Hirano San is very friendly and nice host, he offers good time and money saving advice to us. The apartment is good for family and close to supermarket. I would definitely come back again and recommend to others. 平野先生非常客氣友善，住處環境很好，適合家庭旅遊，我會推薦給其他人。

I had a fantastic stay at Tony’s place in Asakusa! The private apartment was comfortable, and I really appreciated the spacious bathroom layout. Having a separate shower made it easy for multiple people to get ready at the same time. The BNB has a full kitchen, and a dining table separate from where you sleep so it felt very spacious for Tokyo! The only thing to watch out for is that the heating for the room is primarily gas powered so if that is a problem for you keep that in mind.
But the real highlight was Tony himself. He was incredibly kind, provided great local recommendations, and went above and beyond to make sure we were okay. When we accidentally took the wrong train, he stayed up late to ensure we arrived safely and could check in smoothly, despite our very late arrival at 23:30! His hospitality made our trip even more special. Highly recommend staying here!

My friends and I had an amazing experience staying at Hirano San's place! He was so welcoming and always checked in on us to make sure we were enjoying our time and well taken care of. I can't recommend this place enough, very close to Senso-ji temple.

Hiro est très accueillant et très agréable
Super séjour dans un Tokyo magnifique et exceptionnel

Первый раз приехала с детьми в Японию. У Hirano San жили 10 дней. Хозяин отеля очень милый и общительный. Очень помог во многих вопросах, всегда был на связи. Отель очень уютный, есть все самое необходимое и находится в отличном месте. Я очень рада, что нашла этот отель и рада знакомству с таким замечательным Hirano San ☺️🤝 Спасибо большое за все! ❤️
Great and quiet place!
Welcoming and communicative host! Property was as advertised and in a great location for exploring the area. Would rebook in the future!

Le logement de Tony est idéalement situé, à 10/15 minutes à pied du quartier Asakusa très touristique de Tokyo. Les bus passent presque au pied de l'immeuble, ce qui est très pratique.
Tony est toujours souriant et plein de bons conseils, il est à l'écoute et a été aux petits soins pour nous pendant notre séjour de 10 jours.
Le logement est assez spacieux, propre et la vue sur la skytree est superbe. Le quartier est très calme et pourtant très central et proche de tout.
Nous remercions encore Tony pour sa gentillesse et n'hésiterons pas à revenir lors d'un prochain séjour...
Je recommande!
Thanks a lot. a perfect stay in a very calm neighborhood with everything we need at l'essentiel than 15 min walk. the owner is very helpful and welcoming

Memz is a great host, the house is magnificent and the location is very convenient.

This house is big and beautiful - four stories will plenty of room for the individual or a large group. There are several shared bathrooms, two with large tubs and plenty of hot water for a nice soak. The kitchen is easy to use with plenty of cookware and room in the fridge.

A stone's throw from the train station, this house was easy to find and just a short walk. This thriving area of Tokyo provided plenty of entertainment. Memz even accompanied me to a quaint neighborhood bar where I obtained a memorable photo of the locals - kanpai!

Memz sent me a very detailed set of directions to the house and it was really conveniently located for me as I needed to get to Haneda airport the next morning. The house was well organised and clean and had everything I needed. It was quite expensive for what it was by international standards by Japanese prices are higher in general.

My time at Memz's wonderfully spacious, unique, and welcoming abode was far too brief. I got to stay for two nights, the atmosphere was that of a home away from home. Memz was the most gracious host, and an all around interesting a d kind guy! Can't recommend this spot more!
Nice House,quiet area,tatami room,I like there very much

Fantastic place! Huge and welcoming and comfortable. great location too.
Memz is the coolest airbnb host we have ever met! Super friendly, helpful and generous . Perfect English and great tips on where to go in the area. He and Taki. even met us at the station and walked us over to his place, then helped us get settled in. Great host!
Caleb
I had a wonderful stay! The atmosphere is very warm and welcoming.
